description: Evaluates code for security best practices and compliance requirements.

criteria.name: authentication_authorization
criteria.description: Access control is properly implemented and enforced.

scoring.5: All endpoints enforce authentication. Authorization checks verify resource ownership. No privilege escalation paths.
scoring.4: Auth properly implemented with minor gaps in edge case authorization.
scoring.3: Basic auth present but some endpoints or operations lack proper authorization checks.
scoring.2: Auth inconsistently applied. Some endpoints accessible without proper credentials.
scoring.1: No authentication or authorization. Resources accessible by anyone.

criteria.name: injection_prevention
criteria.description: Code is protected against injection attacks (SQL, NoSQL, command, XSS).

scoring.5: All queries use parameterized statements. All user input sanitized for output context. No raw string interpolation in queries or commands.
scoring.4: Parameterized queries used consistently. Minor gaps in output encoding.
scoring.3: Most queries parameterized but some string interpolation present.
scoring.2: Inconsistent use of parameterized queries. Some injection vectors present.
scoring.1: Raw string concatenation in queries or commands. Clear injection vulnerabilities.

criteria.name: secret_management
criteria.description: Secrets are stored securely and never exposed in code, logs, or errors.

scoring.5: No secrets in code. Credentials from Secrets Manager/SSM. Secrets excluded from logs and error responses.
scoring.4: Secrets properly managed with minor logging gaps.
scoring.3: Secrets not in code but may appear in verbose logs or error messages.
scoring.2: Some secrets hard-coded or in configuration files.
scoring.1: Secrets visible in source code, logs, or error responses.

criteria.name: data_protection
criteria.description: Sensitive data is encrypted in transit and at rest, and PII is handled appropriately.

scoring.5: All data encrypted in transit (TLS). Sensitive data encrypted at rest. PII identified and handled per policy. No unnecessary data retention.
scoring.4: Encryption properly configured. Minor gaps in PII handling.
scoring.3: Basic encryption present. PII handling incomplete.
scoring.2: Encryption inconsistent. Some sensitive data unprotected.
scoring.1: No encryption. Sensitive data stored or transmitted in plaintext.

criteria.name: least_privilege
criteria.description: IAM roles and permissions follow least-privilege principle.

scoring.5: All IAM policies scoped to specific resources and actions. No wildcard permissions. Roles are service-specific.
scoring.4: Mostly least-privilege. Minor over-permission in non-critical areas.
scoring.3: Some wildcard actions or broad resource scopes present.
scoring.2: Over-permissive policies. Wildcard actions on broad resource sets.

criteria.name: error_information_leakage
criteria.description: Error responses do not leak internal implementation details.

scoring.5: All errors return generic messages to clients. Stack traces, file paths, and internal details are logged server-side only.
scoring.4: Errors generally safe. Minor information leakage in edge cases.
scoring.3: Some error responses include internal details like file paths or library names.
scoring.2: Stack traces or database errors exposed to clients in some cases.
scoring.1: Detailed stack traces, SQL errors, or internal paths exposed to clients.

output_format.overall_score: float 0-1 (weighted average of criteria scores normalized to 0-1)
output_format.criteria_scores: object mapping criteria name to { score: 1-5, reasoning: string }
